game_name: Might and Magic II: Gates to Another World

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Core mechanic established from first game; up to 6 player characters + 2 hirelings (8 total)

Class-specific quests unlock later (Knight vs Dread Knight, Paladin vs Frost Dragon, Archer vs Baron Wilfrey, Cleric soul reunion, Sorcerer wizard release, Ninja vs enchantress Dawn, Barbarian vs Brutal Bruno)
Required for specific terrain navigation and dungeon progression; max 2 per character
Colored message hints scattered across land; most quests require puzzle solution over combat
Four elemental planes to traverse as part of main narrative against Sheltem
Access to 9 different centuries; only 2 relevant to main story, 2 to sidequest; required for final boss
Characters die at old age (~75); age reversal spells unreliable; health spa on resort isle can reduce age
High sci-fi departure from fantasy setting; timed encrypted message puzzle required to escape planet

typical_relief_triggers: Character age reduction at health spa
typical_tension_triggers: Monster encounters (1 HP to 64000 HP variation)
Class-restricted dungeon areas forcing party separation
Time-limited encrypted message in final dungeon
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Battle-focused core with narrative puzzle interspersion; tension builds through escalating monster variety and difficulty, relieved by discovery and social activities; late-game transitions to sci-fi puzzle intensity

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Colored nonsensical messages scattered across land; sex-change device in cave; seemingly absurd locations reward exploration
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Humorous and surreal discoveries scattered throughout (drunk mechanics, cupie doll collection, teleportation quirks)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Established party mechanics and class selection; safe introduction to combat and navigation with improved graphics/automap UI
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Introduction of elemental planes, secondary skill requirements (mountaineering), puzzle complexity, class-specific quest gates
difficulty_curve.late_game: Time travel integration, character mortality pressure, class-restricted area traversal forcing party reorganization, escalating monster stats (up to 64000 HP)
notable_spikes: Class-specific quest boss encounters (Dread Knight, Frost Dragon, Baron Wilfrey, enchantress Dawn, Brutal Bruno)
Final spaceship dungeon with timed encrypted message puzzle
Sheltem final boss confrontation requiring time travel

notable_level_patterns.description: Dungeons and quest areas restricted by character class; forces party splitting or character swapping to progress
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Each class has dedicated quest dungeon (Dread Knight domain for Knights, Frost Dragon lair for Paladins, etc.)

notable_level_patterns.description: Colored message hints create meta-puzzle layer requiring player collection and interpretation; most quest content requires puzzle-solving over combat
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Scattered colored messages combine into useful hints; time travel puzzles

notable_level_patterns.description: Mountainous regions require mountaineering skill; certain messages require linguist skill; cartographer skill enables automap
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Entire Cron map structured with skill-gated traversal zones

notable_level_patterns.description: Final dungeon abandons fantasy trappings for spaceship and encrypted message solving under time pressure
